summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that unauthenticated attackers can cause memory exhaustion DoS by sending large multipart text fields to public webhook endpoints.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate that sending a multipart/form-data request with a large text field (500MB) causes significant memory consumption leading to OOM kill or service unavailability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh sent a 500MB text field in multipart request to the webhook endpoint. Container memory consumption increased dramatically, and the service became unresponsive. OOM kill triggered or service hang confirmed via memory monitoring.
verification_method: Memory monitoring script tracks container memory usage before and after sending the exploit request. Verification confirms significant memory increase (>500MB) and service unavailability or OOM kill event.
evidence_produced_by_run: Memory consumption increased by >500MB after multipart request
Service response time degraded significantly or became unresponsive
Container OOM kill event triggered (if memory limits configured)
No maxFieldsSize limit prevented the memory exhaustion

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository, checks out the vulnerable commit, builds the Docker image, and runs the container.
